evals.prompt: Check whether my skill-compare skill's SKILL.md is too long or needs reformatting.
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/audit_format.py against the skill, reports its body line count against the 500-line budget, and notes any structure findings (nested references, missing TOC on reference files >100 lines) as candidates to confirm rather than automatic verdicts. Produces an advisory report and does not edit the skill file.
expectations: Runs or references the deterministic audit_format.py scan rather than eyeballing line counts
States the body line count against the 500-line ceiling explicitly
Does not edit the skill file in this pass

evals.prompt: One of my skills is 700 lines. Decompose it.
evals.expected_output: Reads the skill body, proposes a concrete decomposition plan (which sections move to which named reference files, keeping references one level deep from SKILL.md), and gives the exact scripts/decompose.py commands — but does not run them without the user approving the plan (either via a separate --apply invocation or explicit confirmation).
expectations: Proposes specific section -> target-file mappings, not a generic 'split it up' answer
Keeps proposed reference files one level deep (no reference file pointing to a further reference file)
Does not silently run decompose.py without the plan being approved first

evals.prompt: Audit my skills for format and give me a plan-ready report, including plugin skills.
evals.expected_output: A report with per-skill body-line status, frontmatter validity, and structure findings; a plan-ready decompose command list limited to owned skills; plugin/marketplace skills included as advisory-only and excluded from the actionable command list.
expectations: Output is a report with per-skill size/structure findings, not just a pass/fail
Only owned skills appear in the plan-ready decompose command list
Plugin skills are marked advisory-only, consistent with them being overwritten on update
